relay_relay-get-object.name: relay_relay-get-object
relay_relay-get-object.use: Fetch a single HubSpot CRM record (contact, company, deal, or ticket) by numeric ID; these are external customer records, not team members.

relay_relay-search-objects.name: relay_relay-search-objects
relay_relay-search-objects.params_signature: object_type: 'contacts'|'companies'|'deals'|'tickets', query?: string, filterGroups?: array, limit?: number
relay_relay-search-objects.use: Search HubSpot CRM records by text query or property filters; filterGroups combine with OR, filters within a group with AND; returns up to 100 results.
relay_relay-search-objects.returns: Matching CRM records with id and properties fields

relay_relay-list-objects.name: relay_relay-list-objects
relay_relay-list-objects.use: List HubSpot CRM records of the given type with cursor-based pagination, ordered by ID; returns an optimized default property set.
relay_relay-list-objects.returns: Page of CRM records with ~7-10 properties each and a pagination cursor for fetching subsequent pages.

relay_relay-list-associations.name: relay_relay-list-associations
relay_relay-list-associations.use: List HubSpot CRM associations from a source object to all related objects of a target type; object_id must be a numeric string.
relay_relay-list-associations.returns: Array of associations with target object IDs and association types.

relay_relay-create-association.name: relay_relay-create-association
relay_relay-create-association.params_signature: from_type: 'contacts'|'companies'|'deals'|'tickets', from_id: string, to_type: 'contacts'|'companies'|'deals'|'tickets', to_id: string
relay_relay-create-association.use: Create an association linking two HubSpot CRM objects (e.g., contact to company); IDs must be numeric strings.

relay_relay-get-association-definitions.name: relay_relay-get-association-definitions
relay_relay-get-association-definitions.use: Retrieve HubSpot association type definitions between two object types (e.g., contacts to companies).

relay_relay-create-engagement.name: relay_relay-create-engagement
relay_relay-create-engagement.params_signature: engagement_type: 'NOTE'|'EMAIL'|'TASK'|'MEETING'|'CALL', subject?: string, body?: string, ownerId: number, contactId?: number, companyId?: number, dealId?: number, ticketId?: number
relay_relay-create-engagement.use: Create a HubSpot engagement (note, email, task, meeting, or call); supply at least one association ID (contactId, companyId, dealId, or ticketId).
relay_relay-create-engagement.returns: Created engagement object with id, type, and association details.

relay_relay-get-engagement.name: relay_relay-get-engagement
relay_relay-get-engagement.use: Retrieve full details of a HubSpot engagement by its numeric ID; engagement_id must be a digit string.
relay_relay-get-engagement.returns: Engagement object with all properties (id, type, associations, metadata, timestamps).

relay_relay-update-engagement.name: relay_relay-update-engagement
relay_relay-update-engagement.params_signature: engagement_id: string, subject?: string, body?: string, owner_id?: number, outcome?: string, direction?: string
relay_relay-update-engagement.use: Update an existing HubSpot engagement by numeric ID with new subject, body, owner, outcome, or direction values.

relay_relay-search-engagements.name: relay_relay-search-engagements
relay_relay-search-engagements.params_signature: query?: string, engagement_type?: array<'note'|'email'|'call'|'task'|'meeting'>, start_date?: string, end_date?: string, associated_object_id?: string, object_type?: 'company'|'contact'|'deal'|'ticket', limit?: number, offset?: number
relay_relay-search-engagements.use: Search HubSpot engagements (notes, emails, calls, tasks, meetings) by keyword, type, ISO-8601 date range, or associated object; object_type is required when associated_object_id is set; limit defaults to 50 (max 100).
relay_relay-search-engagements.returns: Matching engagements with full details and associations; see tool response for exact shape.

relay_relay-search-owners.name: relay_relay-search-owners
relay_relay-search-owners.use: Search HubSpot internal users/owners by exact email or partial name/email match to find owner IDs for assigning deals, tasks, or engagements.
relay_relay-search-owners.returns: List of owners with id, email, and display name.

relay_hubspot-get-user-details.name: relay_hubspot-get-user-details
relay_hubspot-get-user-details.use: Retrieve details of a specific HubSpot user by numeric user ID (digits only).
relay_hubspot-get-user-details.returns: User object with fields like id, email, and role

relay_relay-list-properties.name: relay_relay-list-properties
relay_relay-list-properties.use: List all available HubSpot properties defined for a given Relay object type.
relay_relay-list-properties.returns: Array of property definitions (name, label, type, etc.)

relay_relay-get-property.name: relay_relay-get-property
relay_relay-get-property.use: Get HubSpot property details by its internal name (not label) for a given object type.
relay_relay-get-property.returns: Property details object including name, label, type, and field metadata.

relay_relay-create-property.name: relay_relay-create-property
relay_relay-create-property.params_signature: object_type: 'contacts'|'companies'|'deals'|'tickets', name: string, label: string, type: 'string'|'number'|'bool'|'datetime'|'date'|'enumeration', description?: string, options?: array
relay_relay-create-property.use: Create a new custom property on a HubSpot object; name must be lowercase with no spaces, and options is required when type is enumeration.
relay_relay-create-property.returns: Created property object with fields like name, label, type.

relay_relay-update-property.name: relay_relay-update-property
relay_relay-update-property.params_signature: object_type: 'contacts'|'companies'|'deals'|'tickets', property_name: string, label?: string, description?: string
relay_relay-update-property.use: Update the display label or description of an existing custom property on a HubSpot object type via Relay.

relay_relay-list-workflows.name: relay_relay-list-workflows
relay_relay-list-workflows.use: List HubSpot workflows via Relay, with optional pagination.

relay_relay-get-workflow.name: relay_relay-get-workflow
relay_relay-get-workflow.use: Get HubSpot workflow details by ID via Relay; workflow_id must be a numeric string.

relay_hubspot-get-schemas.name: relay_hubspot-get-schemas
relay_hubspot-get-schemas.use: Retrieve all custom object schemas defined in the HubSpot account; use returned objectTypeId and objectType values in subsequent API calls.
relay_hubspot-get-schemas.returns: List of schemas with objectTypeId, objectType, properties, and associations.

relay_hubspot-get-link.name: relay_hubspot-get-link
relay_hubspot-get-link.params_signature: portalId: string, uiDomain: string, pageRequests: array<{pagetype: 'record'|'index', objectTypeId: string, objectId?: string}>
relay_hubspot-get-link.use: Generate HubSpot UI URLs for object index pages or specific records; objectTypeId uses HubSpot codes (0-1 contacts, 0-2 companies, 0-3 deals, 0-5 tickets) and objectId is required for 'record' pages.
relay_hubspot-get-link.returns: Array of generated URLs linking to the requested HubSpot UI pages.

relay_relay-create-contact.name: relay_relay-create-contact
relay_relay-create-contact.params_signature: firstname?: string, lastname?: string, email?: string, phone?: string, jobtitle?: string, company?: string, lifecyclestage?: string, hs_lead_status?: string, hubspot_owner_id?: string, associatedCompanyId?: string
relay_relay-create-contact.use: Create a new HubSpot contact using flat field inputs; phone takes country code format, owner and company association IDs must be numeric strings.

relay_relay-update-contact.name: relay_relay-update-contact
relay_relay-update-contact.params_signature: id: string, firstname?: string, lastname?: string, email?: string, phone?: string, jobtitle?: string, company?: string, lifecyclestage?: string, hs_lead_status?: string, hubspot_owner_id?: string
relay_relay-update-contact.use: Update an existing HubSpot contact by numeric ID with provided field values; company is a text name not an ID, and phone should include country code.

relay_relay-get-contact.name: relay_relay-get-contact
relay_relay-get-contact.use: Fetch a HubSpot contact by numeric ID (digits only) and return all of its properties.
relay_relay-get-contact.returns: Contact object with all properties (id, properties, createdAt, updatedAt).

relay_relay-create-company.name: relay_relay-create-company
relay_relay-create-company.params_signature: name: string, domain?: string, industry?: string, phone?: string, city?: string, state?: string, country?: string, website?: string, description?: string, numberofemployees?: string, annualrevenue?: string, type?: 'Prospect'|'Partner'|'Reseller'|'Vendor'|'Other', hubspot_owner_id?: string
relay_relay-create-company.use: Create a new HubSpot company with flat field inputs; domain excludes http://, numberofemployees and hubspot_owner_id must be numeric strings, industry uses a fixed LinkedIn-style list.
relay_relay-create-company.returns: Created company object with id and properties.

relay_relay-update-company.name: relay_relay-update-company
relay_relay-update-company.params_signature: id: string, name?: string, domain?: string, industry?: string, phone?: string, city?: string, state?: string, country?: string, website?: string, description?: string, numberofemployees?: string, annualrevenue?: string, type?: 'Prospect'|'Partner'|'Reseller'|'Vendor'|'Other', hubspot_owner_id?: string
relay_relay-update-company.use: Update an existing HubSpot company by numeric ID; domain omits http://, numberofemployees and hubspot_owner_id must be numeric strings, industry uses a fixed LinkedIn-style list.
relay_relay-update-company.returns: Updated company record, typically with id and updated properties.

relay_relay-get-company.name: relay_relay-get-company
relay_relay-get-company.use: Retrieve a HubSpot company by its numeric ID (digits only) via Relay, returning all company properties.
relay_relay-get-company.returns: Company object with all HubSpot properties.

relay_relay-create-deal.name: relay_relay-create-deal
relay_relay-create-deal.params_signature: dealname: string, amount?: string, closedate?: string, dealstage: 'appointmentscheduled'|'qualifiedtobuy'|'presentationscheduled'|'decisionmakerboughtin'|'contractsent'|'closedwon'|'closedlost', pipeline: 'default', hubspot_owner_id?: string, description?: string, deal_currency_code?: string, hs_priority?: 'low'|'medium'|'high', dealtype?: 'newbusiness'|'existingbusiness'|'renewal', associatedContactId?: string, associatedCompanyId?: string
relay_relay-create-deal.use: Create a new HubSpot deal with flat field inputs; closedate must be YYYY-MM-DD (or ISO-8601), and ID fields must be numeric strings.

relay_relay-update-deal.name: relay_relay-update-deal
relay_relay-update-deal.params_signature: id: string, dealname?: string, amount?: string, closedate?: string, dealstage?: string, pipeline?: 'default', hubspot_owner_id?: string, description?: string, deal_currency_code?: string, hs_priority?: 'low'|'medium'|'high', dealtype?: 'newbusiness'|'existingbusiness'|'renewal'
relay_relay-update-deal.use: Update an existing HubSpot deal by numeric ID; closedate uses YYYY-MM-DD (or ISO-8601), and dealstage must be a HubSpot pipeline stage key.

relay_relay-get-deal.name: relay_relay-get-deal
relay_relay-get-deal.use: Retrieve a HubSpot deal by its numeric ID (digits only) via Relay, returning all deal properties.
relay_relay-get-deal.returns: Deal object with all properties (e.g. dealname, amount, dealstage, pipeline).

relay_relay-create-ticket.name: relay_relay-create-ticket
relay_relay-create-ticket.params_signature: subject: string, content?: string, hs_ticket_priority?: 'LOW'|'MEDIUM'|'HIGH', hs_pipeline_stage?: '1'|'2'|'3'|'4'|'5', hubspot_owner_id?: string, hs_pipeline?: '0', source_type?: string, associatedContactId?: string, associatedCompanyId?: string
relay_relay-create-ticket.use: Create a HubSpot support ticket; pipeline_stage maps 1=New, 2=Waiting on contact, 3=Waiting on us, 4=In Progress, 5=Closed, and ID fields must be numeric strings.

relay_relay-update-ticket.name: relay_relay-update-ticket
relay_relay-update-ticket.params_signature: id: string, subject?: string, content?: string, hs_ticket_priority?: 'LOW'|'MEDIUM'|'HIGH', hs_pipeline_stage?: '1'|'2'|'3'|'4'|'5', hubspot_owner_id?: string, hs_pipeline?: '0', source_type?: string
relay_relay-update-ticket.use: Update an existing HubSpot ticket by numeric ID; pipeline stages map 1=New, 2=Waiting on contact, 3=Waiting on us, 4=In Progress, 5=Closed.

relay_relay-get-ticket.name: relay_relay-get-ticket
relay_relay-get-ticket.use: Retrieve a HubSpot ticket by its numeric ID (digits only) and return all of its properties.
relay_relay-get-ticket.returns: Ticket object with all properties (e.g. subject, content, status, pipeline).

kernel_atlasUserInfo.name: kernel_atlasUserInfo
kernel_atlasUserInfo.use: Get the current authenticated user's info from Atlas (Kernel Jira integration).

kernel_getAccessibleAtlasResources.name: kernel_getAccessibleAtlasResources
kernel_getAccessibleAtlasResources.use: Retrieve the Atlassian cloudid needed to construct Atlas REST API calls for the Jira kernel.

kernel_getVaultSpaces.name: kernel_getVaultSpaces
kernel_getVaultSpaces.params_signature: cloudId: string, cursor?: string, limit?: number=25
kernel_getVaultSpaces.use: List Vault spaces for a Jira/Atlas Cloud instance; cloudId accepts a UUID or site URL, limit max 250.
kernel_getVaultSpaces.returns: Paginated list of spaces with cursor for next page.

kernel_getVaultPage.name: kernel_getVaultPage
kernel_getVaultPage.use: Retrieve a specific Vault page or live doc (including body content) by its ID from an Atlas Cloud instance; cloudId accepts a UUID or site URL.
kernel_getVaultPage.returns: Page object with id, title, body content, and metadata.

kernel_getPagesInVaultSpace.name: kernel_getPagesInVaultSpace
kernel_getPagesInVaultSpace.params_signature: cloudId: string, spaceId: string, limit?: number=100, cursor?: string, status?: 'draft'|'published'|'edited_unpublished'|'archived'|'deleted', title?: string, sort?: string, type?: 'page'|'folder'|'database', parentId?: string, authorId?: string, emoji?: string, slug?: string, version?: number
kernel_getPagesInVaultSpace.use: List pages in a Vault space with filters; cloudId accepts UUID or site URL, parentId 'null'/empty for root, emoji accepts 'none'/'any'/char, sort defaults to created_at_desc.
kernel_getPagesInVaultSpace.returns: Paginated list of page objects with items and cursor for next page.

kernel_getVaultPageAncestors.name: kernel_getVaultPageAncestors
kernel_getVaultPageAncestors.params_signature: cloudId: string, pageId: string, limit?: number
kernel_getVaultPageAncestors.use: Retrieve all ancestor (parent) pages of a given Confluence page in hierarchical order; cloudId accepts a UUID or Atlas site URL.
kernel_getVaultPageAncestors.returns: List of ancestor page objects with fields like id, title, and parent references.

kernel_getVaultPageFooterComments.name: kernel_getVaultPageFooterComments
kernel_getVaultPageFooterComments.params_signature: cloudId: string, pageId: string, limit?: number, cursor?: string, sort?: 'id'|'-id'|'created-date'|'-created-date'
kernel_getVaultPageFooterComments.use: Retrieve footer comments for a Confluence Vault page; cloudId is a UUID, site URL, or extractable from Atlas URLs, and supports cursor pagination.

kernel_getVaultPageDescendants.name: kernel_getVaultPageDescendants
kernel_getVaultPageDescendants.params_signature: cloudId: string, pageId: string, limit?: number, depth?: number, cursor?: string
kernel_getVaultPageDescendants.use: Retrieve all descendant (child) pages of a Confluence page in the hierarchy; cloudId accepts a UUID or site URL.
kernel_getVaultPageDescendants.returns: Paginated list of descendant pages with IDs, titles, and hierarchy info, plus a cursor for next page.

kernel_createVaultPage.name: kernel_createVaultPage
kernel_createVaultPage.params_signature: cloudId: string, spaceId: string, title?: string, parentId?: string, body: string
kernel_createVaultPage.use: Create a new Confluence Vault page in the given space; body must be markdown, and cloudId accepts a UUID or site URL.

kernel_updateVaultPage.name: kernel_updateVaultPage
kernel_updateVaultPage.params_signature: cloudId: string, pageId: string, title?: string, status?: 'current'|'draft', spaceId?: string, parentId?: string, body: string, version: integer, versionMessage?: string
kernel_updateVaultPage.use: Update an existing Confluence Vault page or Live Doc; body must be markdown and version must match the page's current version number.
kernel_updateVaultPage.returns: Updated page object with id, title, version, and body.

kernel_createVaultFooterComment.name: kernel_createVaultFooterComment
kernel_createVaultFooterComment.params_signature: cloudId: string, pageId?: string, parentCommentId?: string, attachmentId?: string, customContentId?: string, body: string
kernel_createVaultFooterComment.use: Create a footer comment (or reply) on a Confluence/Vault page, blog post, attachment, or custom content; body must be Markdown.
kernel_createVaultFooterComment.returns: Created comment object with id, body, and target references.

kernel_searchVaultUsingCql.name: kernel_searchVaultUsingCql
kernel_searchVaultUsingCql.params_signature: cloudId: string, cql: string, cqlcontext?: string, cursor?: string, expand?: string, limit?: number, prev?: boolean, next?: boolean
kernel_searchVaultUsingCql.use: Search Confluence/Vault content using a CQL query string; cloudId accepts a UUID or site URL, limit defaults to 25 (max 250).
kernel_searchVaultUsingCql.returns: Search results with matching content items and pagination links.

kernel_getKernelIssue.name: kernel_getKernelIssue
kernel_getKernelIssue.params_signature: cloudId: string, issueIdOrKey: string, fields?: array, expand?: string, properties?: array
kernel_getKernelIssue.use: Fetch full details of a Kernel (Jira) issue by its ID or key; cloudId is an Atlas instance UUID or site URL.
kernel_getKernelIssue.returns: Issue object with fields, key, id, and optional expanded properties.

kernel_editKernelIssue.name: kernel_editKernelIssue
kernel_editKernelIssue.params_signature: cloudId: string, issueIdOrKey: string, fields: object
kernel_editKernelIssue.use: Update fields of an existing Jira Kernel issue by id or key; cloudId is an Atlas instance UUID or site URL.

kernel_createKernelIssue.name: kernel_createKernelIssue
kernel_createKernelIssue.params_signature: cloudId: string, projectKey: string, summary: string, issueTypeName?: string, description?: string, parent?: string, assignee_account_id?: string, additional_fields?: object
kernel_createKernelIssue.use: Create a new Kernel (Jira) issue; description uses Markdown, parent takes an issue key/id for Subtasks, and additional_fields handles custom/complex fields.

kernel_getTransitionsForKernelIssue.name: kernel_getTransitionsForKernelIssue
kernel_getTransitionsForKernelIssue.params_signature: cloudId: string, issueIdOrKey: string, expand?: string, transitionId?: string
kernel_getTransitionsForKernelIssue.use: List available workflow transitions for a Kernel (Jira) issue by id or key on the given Atlas cloud instance.
kernel_getTransitionsForKernelIssue.returns: Object with transitions array, each including id, name, and target status.

kernel_transitionKernelIssue.name: kernel_transitionKernelIssue
kernel_transitionKernelIssue.params_signature: cloudId: string, issueIdOrKey: string, transition: object, fields?: object, update?: object
kernel_transitionKernelIssue.use: Transition a Jira issue to a new status using a transition id; use getAccessibleAtlasResources if cloudId is unknown.

kernel_lookupKernelAccountId.name: kernel_lookupKernelAccountId
kernel_lookupKernelAccountId.use: Look up Jira user account IDs matching a search string; cloudId is an Atlas Cloud UUID or site URL (use getAccessibleAtlasResources if unknown).

kernel_searchKernelIssuesUsingJql.name: kernel_searchKernelIssuesUsingJql
kernel_searchKernelIssuesUsingJql.params_signature: cloudId: string, jql: string, maxResults?: number, fields?: array, nextPageToken?: string
kernel_searchKernelIssuesUsingJql.use: Search Jira (Kernel) issues with JQL; statusCategory is unsupported, use status instead; default fields: summary, description, status, issuetype, priority, created.
kernel_searchKernelIssuesUsingJql.returns: List of issues with their fields and a nextPageToken for pagination (default 50, max 100 per page).

kernel_addCommentToKernelIssue.name: kernel_addCommentToKernelIssue
kernel_addCommentToKernelIssue.params_signature: cloudId: string, issueIdOrKey: string, body: string, commentVisibility?: object
kernel_addCommentToKernelIssue.use: Add a comment (Markdown body) to an existing Kernel Jira issue, optionally restricting visibility to a group or role.

kernel_getVisibleKernelProjects.name: kernel_getVisibleKernelProjects
kernel_getVisibleKernelProjects.params_signature: cloudId: string, searchString?: string, startAt?: number=0, maxResults?: number=50
kernel_getVisibleKernelProjects.use: List Jira projects visible to the user (view, browse, edit, or create permission); cloudId accepts a UUID or site URL.
kernel_getVisibleKernelProjects.returns: Paginated list of project objects with fields like id, key, name, and projectTypeKey.

kernel_getKernelProjectIssueTypesMetadat.name: kernel_getKernelProjectIssueTypesMetadata
kernel_getKernelProjectIssueTypesMetadat.params_signature: cloudId: string, projectIdOrKey: string, startAt?: number=0, maxResults?: number=50
kernel_getKernelProjectIssueTypesMetadat.use: Get a page of issue type metadata for a Jira project; cloudId is a UUID, site URL, or extracted from an Atlas URL.
kernel_getKernelProjectIssueTypesMetadat.returns: Paginated list of issue type metadata objects for the project.

kernel_getJiraIssueRemoteIssueLinks.name: kernel_getJiraIssueRemoteIssueLinks
kernel_getJiraIssueRemoteIssueLinks.params_signature: cloudId: string, issueIdOrKey: string, globalId?: string, linkId?: string
kernel_getJiraIssueRemoteIssueLinks.use: Retrieve remote issue links attached to a Jira issue, optionally filtered by globalId or linkId.
kernel_getJiraIssueRemoteIssueLinks.returns: Array of remote link objects with id, globalId, application, and object details.

kernel_getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields.name: kernel_getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields
kernel_getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields.params_signature: cloudId: string, projectKeys?: string, issuetypeIds?: string, issuetypeNames?: string, startAt?: number=0, maxResults?: number=50
kernel_getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields.use: Retrieve Jira issue type metadata including field definitions for a Cloud instance, optionally filtered by project, issue type ID, or name; max 200 per page.
kernel_getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields.returns: Paginated list of issue type metadata entries with their fields; see tool response for exact shape.

latch_fakelook_auth_login.name: latch_fakelook_auth_login
latch_fakelook_auth_login.use: Authenticate a FakeLook user with email and password, recording a login activity for session tracking.
latch_fakelook_auth_login.returns: Authenticated user object and auth state.

latch_fakelook_auth_logout.name: latch_fakelook_auth_logout
latch_fakelook_auth_logout.use: Log out the currently authenticated Latch (Outlook) FakeLook user and clear the session state.
latch_fakelook_auth_logout.returns: Updated auth state indicating the user is logged out.

latch_list_emails.name: latch_list_emails
latch_list_emails.use: List recent Outlook emails from the specified folder (default inbox), newest first; limit defaults to 10, max 100.
latch_list_emails.returns: List of email objects with fields like id, subject, sender, receivedDateTime.

latch_get_conversation_messages.name: latch_get_conversation_messages
latch_get_conversation_messages.params_signature: conversationId: string, limit?: integer, offset?: integer
latch_get_conversation_messages.use: Fetch Outlook conversation thread messages oldest-to-newest via Latch; accepts a conversation ID or any message ID in the thread.
latch_get_conversation_messages.returns: Paginated list of message objects in the conversation; default limit 10, max 100.

latch_list_mail_folders.name: latch_list_mail_folders
latch_list_mail_folders.use: List all mail folders in the authenticated user's Outlook mailbox.
latch_list_mail_folders.returns: Array of mail folder objects (id, displayName, etc.); see tool response.

latch_view_email.name: latch_view_email
latch_view_email.use: Fetch a single Outlook email by ID, optionally marking it as read; returns only that message, not the full thread.
latch_view_email.returns: Email object with fields like id, subject, from, body, receivedDateTime.

latch_search_emails.name: latch_search_emails
latch_search_emails.params_signature: query?: string, folder?: string, from?: string, to?: string, cc?: string, dateFrom?: string, dateTo?: string, readStatus?: 'all'|'read'|'unread', hasAttachments?: boolean, limit?: number
latch_search_emails.use: Search Outlook emails by text, folder, sender/recipient, date range, read status, or attachments; dates are ISO YYYY-MM-DD and email filters require resolved addresses.
latch_search_emails.returns: List of matching email messages (up to limit, default 50, max 100).

latch_send_email.name: latch_send_email
latch_send_email.params_signature: to: array, subject: string, body: string, cc?: array, bcc?: array, saveAsDraft?: boolean, importance?: 'low'|'normal'|'high'
latch_send_email.use: Send or draft an Outlook email via Latch to the given recipients.

latch_create_reply_draft.name: latch_create_reply_draft
latch_create_reply_draft.params_signature: messageId: string, message: string, cc?: array, bcc?: array
latch_create_reply_draft.use: Create a draft reply to an Outlook email message via Latch without sending it.

latch_create_reply_all_draft.name: latch_create_reply_all_draft
latch_create_reply_all_draft.params_signature: messageId: string, message: string, cc?: array, bcc?: array
latch_create_reply_all_draft.use: Create an Outlook draft reply-all to the specified email message without sending it, via Latch.

latch_create_forward_draft.name: latch_create_forward_draft
latch_create_forward_draft.params_signature: messageId: string, message?: string, toRecipients: array, cc?: array, bcc?: array
latch_create_forward_draft.use: Create an Outlook draft forwarding the specified email to given recipients without sending it.
latch_create_forward_draft.returns: Draft message object, typically including id and recipient fields.

latch_reply_to_email.name: latch_reply_to_email
latch_reply_to_email.params_signature: messageId: string, message: string, cc?: array, bcc?: array, replyAll?: boolean
latch_reply_to_email.use: Reply to an Outlook email message; set replyAll=true to reply to all recipients (default false replies only to sender).

latch_forward_email.name: latch_forward_email
latch_forward_email.params_signature: messageId: string, message?: string, toRecipients: array<string>
latch_forward_email.use: Forward an Outlook email message to one or more recipients, optionally including additional message content.

latch_send_draft.name: latch_send_draft
latch_send_draft.use: Send a previously created Outlook draft message via Latch.

latch_mark_email_read.name: latch_mark_email_read
latch_mark_email_read.use: Mark one or more Outlook emails as read (isRead=true) or unread (isRead=false); supports bulk action via multiple message IDs.

latch_delete_email.name: latch_delete_email
latch_delete_email.use: Delete one or more Outlook emails by ID via Latch; messages outside the deleted folder are moved there, messages already in it are permanently deleted.

latch_move_email.name: latch_move_email
latch_move_email.use: Move one or more Outlook emails to another folder; server validates allowed source→destination pairs, and 'sent' cannot be a destination.

latch_copy_email.name: latch_copy_email
latch_copy_email.use: Copy one or more Outlook emails to another folder; destinationId must be one of 'inbox','sent','drafts','deleted','junk','archive','outbox'.

latch_archive_email.name: latch_archive_email
latch_archive_email.use: Archive one or more Outlook emails by moving them to the Archive folder; cannot archive messages already in Archive or in Drafts.

latch_flag_email.name: latch_flag_email
latch_flag_email.use: Flag, unflag, or mark-complete one or more Outlook emails for follow-up in a single bulk action via Latch.

latch_list_email_attachments.name: latch_list_email_attachments
latch_list_email_attachments.use: List all attachments for an Outlook email message via Latch.
latch_list_email_attachments.returns: Array of attachment metadata objects for the specified message.

latch_add_email_attachment.name: latch_add_email_attachment
latch_add_email_attachment.params_signature: messageId: string, name: string, textContent?: string, htmlContent?: string, contentBytes?: string, contentType?: string, isInline?: boolean
latch_add_email_attachment.use: Add an attachment to an Outlook draft message; provide exactly one of textContent, htmlContent, or base64 contentBytes, and contentType auto-detects from extension.

latch_get_email_attachment.name: latch_get_email_attachment
latch_get_email_attachment.params_signature: messageId: string, attachmentId?: string, attachmentName?: string, includeContent?: boolean
latch_get_email_attachment.use: Download an Outlook email attachment by message ID; auto-downloads if only one attachment exists, otherwise specify attachmentId or attachmentName (partial, case-insensitive).
latch_get_email_attachment.returns: Attachment metadata and base64 content, or a list of attachments when multiple exist and none specified.

latch_get_email_mime.name: latch_get_email_mime
latch_get_email_mime.use: Retrieve the raw MIME representation of an Outlook email message via Latch.

latch_get_email_delta.name: latch_get_email_delta
latch_get_email_delta.params_signature: deltaToken?: string, deltaLink?: string, folderId?: string
latch_get_email_delta.use: Fetch Outlook email changes since last sync; omit tokens for initial sync, then pass returned deltaToken on subsequent calls, optionally scoped to a folder ('inbox','sent','drafts','deleted','junk','archive','outbox').
latch_get_email_delta.returns: Changed messages plus a new deltaToken/deltaLink to use in the next sync call.

latch_list_calendar_events.name: latch_list_calendar_events
latch_list_calendar_events.params_signature: startDateTime?: string, endDateTime?: string, calendarIds?: array
latch_list_calendar_events.use: List Outlook calendar events within an ISO-8601 date range, optionally filtered to specific calendar IDs; defaults to now through one week ahead.
latch_list_calendar_events.returns: Array of calendar event objects; see tool response for fields.

latch_create_calendar_event.name: latch_create_calendar_event
latch_create_calendar_event.params_signature: subject: string, startDateTime: string, endDateTime: string, calendarId?: string, location?: string, body?: string, attendees?: array, isAllDay?: boolean, isInPersonMeeting?: boolean, importance?: 'low'|'normal'|'high', showAs?: 'free'|'tentative'|'busy'|'oof'|'workingElsewhere', reminderMinutesBeforeStart?: number=15, responseRequested?: boolean, allowForwarding?: boolean, categories?: array, isRecurring?: boolean, recurrenceType?: 'daily'|'weekly'|'monthly'|'yearly', recurrenceInterval?: integer=1, recurrenceDaysOfWeek?: array, recurrenceEndDate?: string
latch_create_calendar_event.use: Create an Outlook calendar event; startDateTime/endDateTime use ISO-8601, recurrenceEndDate uses YYYY-MM-DD, and defaults to an online meeting unless isInPersonMeeting is true.

latch_get_calendar_event.name: latch_get_calendar_event
latch_get_calendar_event.use: Retrieve details of a specific Outlook calendar event by its ID via Latch.
latch_get_calendar_event.returns: Event object with fields like subject, start, end, attendees, location.

latch_update_calendar_event.name: latch_update_calendar_event
latch_update_calendar_event.params_signature: eventId: string, calendarId?: string, subject?: string, startDateTime?: string, endDateTime?: string, location?: string, body?: string, attendees?: array, isAllDay?: boolean, isInPersonMeeting?: boolean, importance?: 'low'|'normal'|'high', showAs?: 'free'|'tentative'|'busy'|'oof'|'workingElsewhere', reminderMinutesBeforeStart?: number, responseRequested?: boolean, allowForwarding?: boolean, categories?: array, isRecurring?: boolean, recurrenceType?: 'daily'|'weekly'|'monthly'|'yearly', recurrenceInterval?: integer, recurrenceDaysOfWeek?: array, recurrenceEndDate?: string
latch_update_calendar_event.use: Update an existing Outlook calendar event; date-times use ISO-8601, recurrenceEndDate uses YYYY-MM-DD, and categories/calendarId come from their respective list tools.

latch_delete_calendar_event.name: latch_delete_calendar_event
latch_delete_calendar_event.use: Delete an Outlook calendar event via Latch by its event ID.

latch_list_calendars.name: latch_list_calendars
latch_list_calendars.use: List all calendars in the authenticated user's Outlook mailbox via Latch.
latch_list_calendars.returns: Array of calendar objects (id, name, etc.); see tool response

latch_create_calendar.name: latch_create_calendar
latch_create_calendar.use: Create a new Outlook calendar; color accepts a hex value (e.g. '#9d2c2c') or a named preset like darkRed, lightBlue, etc.

latch_get_calendar_colors.name: latch_get_calendar_colors
latch_get_calendar_colors.use: List available Outlook calendar color options with their hex values, matching the UI color picker.
latch_get_calendar_colors.returns: Array of color entries with name and hex value.

latch_get_calendar.name: latch_get_calendar
latch_get_calendar.use: Get details of a specific Outlook calendar via Latch.
latch_get_calendar.returns: Calendar object with fields such as id, name, owner, and color.

latch_update_calendar.name: latch_update_calendar
latch_update_calendar.params_signature: calendarId: string, name?: string, color?: string
latch_update_calendar.use: Update an existing Outlook calendar's name or color via Latch.

latch_delete_calendar.name: latch_delete_calendar
latch_delete_calendar.use: Delete an Outlook calendar via Latch.

latch_get_calendar_categories.name: latch_get_calendar_categories
latch_get_calendar_categories.use: List all available Outlook calendar categories (tags) that can be assigned to events.
latch_get_calendar_categories.returns: Array of category objects, typically with name and color fields.

latch_accept_meeting.name: latch_accept_meeting
latch_accept_meeting.use: Accept an Outlook meeting invitation via Latch; errors if the event is missing, the user isn't an attendee, or the user is the organizer.

latch_decline_meeting.name: latch_decline_meeting
latch_decline_meeting.use: Decline an Outlook meeting invitation; errors if the event is missing, the user isn't an attendee, or the user is the organizer.

latch_tentatively_accept_meeting.name: latch_tentatively_accept_meeting
latch_tentatively_accept_meeting.use: Tentatively accept an Outlook meeting invitation; errors if the event doesn't exist, the user isn't an attendee, or the user is the organizer.

latch_cancel_calendar_event.name: latch_cancel_calendar_event
latch_cancel_calendar_event.params_signature: eventId: string, comment?: string, sendCancellation?: boolean
latch_cancel_calendar_event.use: Cancel an Outlook calendar event via Latch, marking it cancelled without deletion and optionally notifying attendees (default true).

latch_list_email_categories.name: latch_list_email_categories
latch_list_email_categories.use: List all email categories defined in the user's Outlook mailbox via Latch.
latch_list_email_categories.returns: Array of category objects (e.g. name, color, id).

latch_get_email_category.name: latch_get_email_category
latch_get_email_category.use: Retrieve details of a specific Outlook email category by its numeric ID via Latch.

latch_create_email_category.name: latch_create_email_category
latch_create_email_category.use: Create a new Outlook email category via Latch; color accepts preset values 'preset0' through 'preset24'.

latch_list_contacts.name: latch_list_contacts
latch_list_contacts.use: List contacts in the user's Outlook mailbox, optionally filtering by name or email; count defaults to 50, max 250.
latch_list_contacts.returns: Array of contact objects with name and email fields.

latch_get_contact.name: latch_get_contact
latch_get_contact.use: Retrieve details of a specific Outlook contact by its ID via Latch.
latch_get_contact.returns: Contact object with fields such as id, displayName, emailAddresses, and phone numbers.

latch_create_contact.name: latch_create_contact
latch_create_contact.params_signature: displayName: string, givenName?: string, surname?: string, emailAddresses?: array, businessPhones?: array, mobilePhone?: string, companyName?: string, department?: string, officeLocation?: string, jobTitle?: string, personalNotes?: string
latch_create_contact.use: Create a new Outlook contact via Latch; emailAddresses items are objects with required 'address' and optional 'name'.

latch_update_contact.name: latch_update_contact
latch_update_contact.params_signature: contactId: string, displayName?: string, givenName?: string, surname?: string, emailAddresses?: array, businessPhones?: array, mobilePhone?: string, companyName?: string, department?: string, officeLocation?: string, jobTitle?: string, personalNotes?: string
latch_update_contact.use: Update an existing Outlook contact via Latch; only provided fields are modified.

latch_delete_contact.name: latch_delete_contact
latch_delete_contact.use: Delete an Outlook contact via Latch by its contact ID.

latch_search_people.name: latch_search_people
latch_search_people.use: Search Outlook for people by name or email, ranked by relevance based on email history, shared events, and organization.
latch_search_people.returns: List of people with name, email, and relevance details

latch_list_todo_lists.name: latch_list_todo_lists
latch_list_todo_lists.use: List the current user's Microsoft To Do task lists via Latch (Outlook); defaults to 50 lists, max 200.

latch_get_todo_list.name: latch_get_todo_list
latch_get_todo_list.use: Fetch a single Outlook To-Do task list by ID along with its tasks.
latch_get_todo_list.returns: List object with tasks array containing title, dueDateTime, importance, and status.

latch_create_todo_list.name: latch_create_todo_list
latch_create_todo_list.use: Create a new Outlook To-Do task list with the given display name.
latch_create_todo_list.returns: Created list object with id and displayName.

latch_update_todo_list.name: latch_update_todo_list
latch_update_todo_list.use: Rename an existing Outlook To-Do task list.

latch_delete_todo_list.name: latch_delete_todo_list
latch_delete_todo_list.use: Delete an Outlook To-Do task list via Latch.

latch_list_todo_tasks.name: latch_list_todo_tasks
latch_list_todo_tasks.params_signature: listId?: string, limit?: integer=50, offset?: integer=0, status?: integer, importance?: integer, sortBy?: 'createdDateTime'|'dueDateTime'|'importance'|'title', sortDirection?: 'asc'|'desc'
latch_list_todo_tasks.use: List tasks in an Outlook To-Do list, defaulting to the 'Tasks' list; status 0=notStarted/1=completed, importance 0=normal/1=high filtered client-side.
latch_list_todo_tasks.returns: Array of task objects with fields like id, title, status, importance, createdDateTime, dueDateTime.

latch_get_todo_task.name: latch_get_todo_task
latch_get_todo_task.use: Retrieve a single Outlook To Do task by ID; if listId is omitted, the default 'Tasks' list is used.
latch_get_todo_task.returns: Task object with fields such as id, title, status, and dueDateTime.

latch_create_todo_task.name: latch_create_todo_task
latch_create_todo_task.params_signature: listId?: string, title: string, body?: string, importance?: integer, status?: integer, dueDateTime?: string, reminderDateTime?: string, recurrence?: 'daily'|'weekdays'|'weekly'|'monthly'|'yearly', categories?: array
latch_create_todo_task.use: Create an Outlook To-Do task; omit listId to use/create the default 'Tasks' list, dates are UTC ISO-8601, importance/status are 0|1, categories accept 'myDay:YYYY-MM-DD'.

latch_update_todo_task.name: latch_update_todo_task
latch_update_todo_task.params_signature: listId?: string, taskId: string, title?: string, body?: string, importance?: integer, status?: integer, dueDateTime?: string, reminderDateTime?: string, recurrence?: 'daily'|'weekdays'|'weekly'|'monthly'|'yearly', categories?: array
latch_update_todo_task.use: Update an Outlook To-Do task; importance/status use 0/1 ints, date fields are UTC ISO-8601, set reminderDateTime/recurrence to null to clear, categories accept color names or 'myDay:YYYY-MM-DD'.

latch_delete_todo_task.name: latch_delete_todo_task
latch_delete_todo_task.use: Delete a task from an Outlook To Do list via Latch; defaults to the 'Tasks' list when listId is omitted.

latch_list_todo_steps.name: latch_list_todo_steps
latch_list_todo_steps.use: List steps (subtasks) of an Outlook To Do task; defaults to the 'Tasks' list if listId is omitted.
latch_list_todo_steps.returns: Array of step objects with fields like id, displayName, and isChecked.

latch_create_todo_step.name: latch_create_todo_step
latch_create_todo_step.params_signature: listId?: string, taskId: string, displayName: string, isChecked?: boolean
latch_create_todo_step.use: Create a step (subtask) under an Outlook to-do task; if listId is omitted, the default 'Tasks' list is used.

latch_get_todo_step.name: latch_get_todo_step
latch_get_todo_step.use: Fetch a single Latch (Outlook) todo step by its ID.

latch_update_todo_step.name: latch_update_todo_step
latch_update_todo_step.params_signature: stepId: string, displayName?: string, isChecked?: boolean
latch_update_todo_step.use: Update a todo step's name or checked status by its step ID in Latch (Outlook).

latch_delete_todo_step.name: latch_delete_todo_step
latch_delete_todo_step.use: Delete a Latch (Outlook) todo step by its ID.

latch_get_todo_counts.name: latch_get_todo_counts
latch_get_todo_counts.use: Get smart-list and per-list todo counts for Latch (Outlook), matching the client UI.

latch_create_task_from_email.name: latch_create_task_from_email
latch_create_task_from_email.use: Create a Microsoft To-Do task from a flagged Outlook email, mirroring the client's flagged-email integration.

latch_list_todo_smart_list_tasks.name: latch_list_todo_smart_list_tasks
latch_list_todo_smart_list_tasks.params_signature: smartListType: 'myday'|'important'|'planned'|'tasks'|'flagged', limit?: integer, offset?: integer, orderby?: string
latch_list_todo_smart_list_tasks.use: List Outlook To Do tasks for a smart list (myday, important, planned, tasks, or flagged), including active and completed; default limit 50, default sort createdDateTime desc.
latch_list_todo_smart_list_tasks.returns: Task list with fields like id, title, status, importance, dueDateTime; planned type groups by earlier/today/tomorrow/next5days/later.

latch_list_teams.name: latch_list_teams
latch_list_teams.use: List all Microsoft Teams the current user is a member of via the Latch Outlook integration.
latch_list_teams.returns: Array of teams with name, description, and id fields.

latch_get_team.name: latch_get_team
latch_get_team.use: Retrieve detailed information about a specific Microsoft Team via Latch (Outlook).
latch_get_team.returns: Team object with displayName, description, visibility, and other settings.

latch_list_team_members.name: latch_list_team_members
latch_list_team_members.use: List all members of a specific Microsoft Team via Latch (Outlook).
latch_list_team_members.returns: Array of team member objects; see tool response

latch_list_team_channels.name: latch_list_team_channels
latch_list_team_channels.use: List all channels within a specified Microsoft Team via Latch (Outlook).
latch_list_team_channels.returns: Array of channel objects with fields like id, displayName, and description.

latch_get_channel.name: latch_get_channel
latch_get_channel.use: Retrieve detailed information about a specific channel within a Microsoft Teams team via Latch (Outlook).
latch_get_channel.returns: Channel object with displayName, description, membershipType, and webUrl.

latch_create_channel.name: latch_create_channel
latch_create_channel.params_signature: teamId: string, displayName: string, description?: string, membershipType?: 'standard'|'private'|'shared'
latch_create_channel.use: Create a new channel in a Microsoft Team via Outlook Latch; displayName is limited to 50 characters and membershipType defaults to standard.

latch_get_channel_messages.name: latch_get_channel_messages
latch_get_channel_messages.params_signature: teamId: string, channelId: string, limit?: number
latch_get_channel_messages.use: Retrieve recent messages from a specific channel in a Microsoft Team via Outlook/Latch; limit defaults to 20, max 50.

latch_get_channel_message.name: latch_get_channel_message
latch_get_channel_message.params_signature: teamId: string, channelId: string, messageId: string
latch_get_channel_message.use: Retrieve a specific message from a Microsoft Teams channel (via Latch/Outlook) by its ID, including content, sender, and attachments.
latch_get_channel_message.returns: Message object with content, sender, attachments, and metadata.

latch_send_channel_message.name: latch_send_channel_message
latch_send_channel_message.params_signature: teamId: string, channelId: string, message: string
latch_send_channel_message.use: Send a message to a specific channel in a Microsoft Team via Latch (Outlook).

latch_update_channel_message.name: latch_update_channel_message
latch_update_channel_message.params_signature: teamId: string, channelId: string, messageId: string, content: string, contentType?: 'text'|'html'
latch_update_channel_message.use: Edit the content of an existing Teams channel message via Latch (Outlook); only the original sender can update their own messages.

latch_delete_channel_message.name: latch_delete_channel_message
latch_delete_channel_message.params_signature: teamId: string, channelId: string, messageId: string
latch_delete_channel_message.use: Soft delete a message from a Latch (Outlook) channel, marking it as deleted without permanent removal.

latch_list_drives.name: latch_list_drives
latch_list_drives.use: List all SharePoint document libraries (drives) accessible to the current user via their Team memberships in Latch (Outlook).
latch_list_drives.returns: Array of drive objects associated with the user's Teams.

latch_list_drive_items.name: latch_list_drive_items
latch_list_drive_items.params_signature: driveId: string, folderId?: string, top?: number, skip?: number
latch_list_drive_items.use: List files and folders in a SharePoint drive via Outlook/Latch; omit folderId or pass 'root' for the drive root.
latch_list_drive_items.returns: Array of drive items (files/folders) with metadata; see tool response.

latch_get_drive_item.name: latch_get_drive_item
latch_get_drive_item.use: Retrieve detailed metadata for a specific file or folder in a SharePoint drive via Latch (Outlook).
latch_get_drive_item.returns: Drive item object with fields like id, name, size, and type.

latch_upload_drive_item.name: latch_upload_drive_item
latch_upload_drive_item.params_signature: driveId: string, parentId: string, fileName: string, content: string, contentType?: string
latch_upload_drive_item.use: Upload a base64-encoded file (max 5MB) to a SharePoint drive folder; use 'root' as parentId for the drive root.
latch_upload_drive_item.returns: Uploaded drive item metadata (id, name, size, etc.)

latch_update_drive_item.name: latch_update_drive_item
latch_update_drive_item.params_signature: driveId: string, itemId: string, name?: string, newParentId?: string
latch_update_drive_item.use: Rename or move a file/folder in a SharePoint drive via Latch (Outlook); supply name to rename, newParentId to move, or both.
latch_update_drive_item.returns: Updated drive item object (id, name, parent info).

latch_delete_drive_item.name: latch_delete_drive_item
latch_delete_drive_item.use: Soft-delete a file or folder from a SharePoint drive via Outlook Latch; deleted items may be recoverable.

latch_create_folder.name: latch_create_folder
latch_create_folder.params_signature: driveId: string, parentId: string, name: string
latch_create_folder.use: Create a new folder in a SharePoint drive; pass 'root' as parentId to create at the drive root.
